skill.name: draw
skill.description: Create, edit, and convert vector drawings and diagrams with LibreOffice Draw workflows.
skill.summary: Automates LibreOffice Draw diagram creation and ODG, SVG, PDF, and PNG conversion workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified shell command examples in SKILL.md, including soffice conversion commands, pip installation commands, and a process termination command. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious network behavior was found; weak cryptography and network reconnaissance alerts appear to be false positives from ordinary Draw documentation and local LibreOffice automation examples.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Automation Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill documents command-line use of LibreOffice, package installation commands, and batch conversion loops. These are legitimate for a Draw automation skill, but they require command execution on local files and should be reviewed before use with untrusted paths or inputs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and central to the documented workflow. The risk is moderate because they appear task-related rather than malicious, but they still execute local programs and process user files.

medium_findings.title: Process Termination Command in Troubleshooting
medium_findings.description: The troubleshooting section recommends terminating LibreOffice processes before starting a headless listener. This is not malicious, but it can disrupt active user sessions or unsaved work if executed without confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The process termination command is directly present in the troubleshooting workflow. The impact depends on the local environment, so the confidence is high for operational risk but not for malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Local UNO Socket Usage
low_findings.description: The skill shows LibreOffice UNO automation over localhost. This is expected for headless LibreOffice control and is not network reconnaissance, but it opens or connects to a local automation service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The socket target is localhost and matches normal LibreOffice UNO usage. It remains a low operational concern because local services can expose automation surfaces if left running.

low_findings.title: Benign Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to LibreOffice documentation, the LibreOffice API reference, and the SVG specification. No evidence found of tracking endpoints, exfiltration, or suspicious domains.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed domains are public documentation resources aligned with the skill purpose. Static network flags are false positives in this context.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at the description and capability text. No cryptographic code, hashing code, encryption API, or credential handling was found in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines describe vector graphics and technical drawings, not cryptography. This is a clear scanner false positive based on the reviewed file content.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on shell commands for LibreOffice conversion, package installation, and headless automation. Commands should use explicit filenames, quoted paths, and user confirmation for destructive or broad operations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command execution pattern is documented repeatedly and intentionally. The danger is contextual because the commands support legitimate graphics workflows.

content.value_statement: Manual diagram creation and file conversion can slow office and documentation work. This skill guides LibreOffice Draw workflows for vector graphics, diagrams, and common export formats.
actual_capabilities: Create new LibreOffice Draw ODG files and diagram structures.
Convert ODG drawings to SVG, PDF, PNG, JPG, and related graphics formats.
Describe command-line workflows using soffice for headless file conversion.
Guide Python UNO automation for creating and saving drawing documents.
Support batch conversion and template-based diagram production.
limitations: Requires LibreOffice and relevant Python libraries to be installed locally.
Does not include a complete reusable script or packaged conversion tool.
Headless conversion quality can vary by source document and export filter.
Commands should be reviewed before running on untrusted files or broad directories.

use_cases.title: Generate process diagrams
use_cases.description: Create ODG workflow diagrams and export them for reports, wikis, or presentations.

use_cases.title: Convert drawing archives
use_cases.description: Batch convert existing LibreOffice Draw files into PDF or SVG for distribution.

use_cases.title: Automate diagram templates
use_cases.description: Use UNO concepts to create repeatable drawing documents from structured information.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new drawing plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a LibreOffice Draw plan for a simple onboarding workflow with five steps and clear labels.

prompt_templates.title: Convert an ODG file
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the safest LibreOffice Draw command workflow to convert one ODG file to PDF and SVG.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare batch exports
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a batch conversion workflow for a folder of ODG diagrams that exports PDFs and keeps source files unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Automate drawing generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Outline a Python UNO approach for generating Draw diagrams from structured process data and saving an ODG master file.

output_examples.input: A request to convert a Draw file to web and print formats.
output_examples.output: A short workflow that checks the source file, runs LibreOffice conversion for SVG and PDF, and verifies the exported files.

output_examples.input: A request to create a technical architecture diagram.
output: A suggested page layout with layers.
A list of shapes and labels to create.
Export recommendations for ODG, SVG, and PDF.

output_examples.input: A request to troubleshoot a failed headless export.
output_examples.output: A diagnostic checklist covering LibreOffice availability, file permissions, headless mode, and export filter settings.

best_practices: Keep original ODG files under version control before exporting derived formats.
Use quoted, explicit file paths when running conversion commands.
Test conversion output visually before sharing diagrams externally.
anti_patterns: Do not run batch conversion commands in broad directories without reviewing the matched files.
Do not leave a headless UNO listener running longer than needed.
Do not rely on exported PNG files as the only editable diagram source.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create LibreOffice Draw diagrams, vector graphics, technical drawings, and export workflows.

faq.question: Can it convert ODG files to PDF?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents LibreOffice command-line conversion from ODG to PDF and other graphics formats.

faq.question: Does it require LibreOffice?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflows depend on LibreOffice Draw and the soffice command-line tool.

faq.question: Can it automate Draw with Python?
faq.answer: Yes. It introduces Python UNO automation for creating and saving drawing documents.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted files?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review commands and isolate untrusted documents before running conversions or automation.

faq.question: Does it include complete production scripts?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and examples, not a packaged production automation library.
